sutta:
the buddha said monks may be a believer but yet not be virtuous thus in that respect he is incomplete that the fact must be remedied by the thought how can i be both a believer and virtuous but monks when among his both of these he is complete in that respect stop here for a moment here to be a believer a mum may be a believer yeah actually you could say a mom may have faith and yet not be virtuous and yet not have moral conduct so that monk has faith but he that's only what does the only thing he has so he has to develop moral conduct then he is complete up to that respect and the buddha continued monks among may be both a believer and virtuous yet not learned he may be a believer virtuous and learner yet no dumb preacher he may be a believer virtuous learner and a dharma preacher yet not a frequenter of debates he may be all of these yet not be confident in expounding dhamma in the company he may be all of these yet not expert in monastic discipline vinaya he may be all of these yet no forest dweller a lodger in solitude he may be all of these yet not be 1 who attains at will without difficulty and without trouble the 4 jaanas which belong to the higher mind which even in the same visible state are blissful to abide in nor does he by destroying the ah in the same visible state comprehending it of himself realize the release by mind the release by wisdom and attaining it abide therein so in that respect he is incomplete that defect must be remedied by the thor how can i be both a believer and virtuous also learner a dhamma preacher a frequenter of debates and confident in expounding dhamma in a company also expert in the monastic discipline a forest dweller a lodger in solitude and 1 who attains at will the 4 jaanas and by destroying the ask of us realize the release by mind the release by wisdom and attaining it abide therein then monks when he has attained each 1 of these 10 things he is complete in that respect monks if he be endowed with these 10 qualities among us all together charming and complete in every attribute that's the end of the shutdown so you find this suta is giving practical advice to monks to attain these various qualities that make for veneration and some of these qualities are also repeated elsewhere so these qualities are very good qualities that the mom should develop the first 1 he should have faith having faith means having faith in the buddha dharma and sangha a triple gem having faith in the buddha means having faith or believing that the buddha is enlightened and then having faith in the dhamma means believing in the things that aren't taught in the dhamma having faith that they are the words of an enlightened person and then having faith in the sangha [Music] sometimes people think that the sangha only refers to arya but actually you find in like the majima nikaya i think suta number 142 [Music] the buddha says that the sangha refers to the community of monks or nuns so because if you take refuge with the buddha dharma and sangha if you just take refuge with the arya sangra who is [Music] in the suttas for example some anagami lay disciples like chitta or hataka when monks come to the house to arms around and they pay respect to the monk and everything like that because the monk has renounced and he represents the enunciate disciples of the buddha so they pay respect to the monk so that is a having faith and then the second 1 having moral conduct having moral conduct is keeping the precepts and basically the precepts that are important are those precepts which [Music] help us not to harm other living beings and then number 3 is learning sometimes they say much learning their bollywood is bahu sometimes the equivalent is sacha bahu means much means hearing much hearing of the words of the buddha and bahu sacha is much much hearing of the sacha's truth truths means the truths found in the dhamma or the buddha's words so it also means the same thing being knowledgeable about the buddha's teachings the buddha's words in particular later we'll find some others some of these terms are explained in more detail the dhamma preacher so after a month uh has faith then he keeps his uh sheila then after he has seen he should be learned in the buddha's teachings and after he's learned it then he should try to teach the buddha's teachings to others like a tama preacher and after that he should be a frequenter of debates actually here frequenter of debates he could also say that he is 1 who discusses the dhamma with others so because discussion of dhamma is also very important to help us understand the dharma sometimes there are certain points of dhamma when we read the suttas we are not very clear so when we discuss with other people who also study the sutras then sometimes they might understand certain topics a bit better than us and we could learn something from them as they say more aids are better than 1 so here the number 5 is frequenter of debates or frequent or dharma discussions and then after that confident in expounding dhamma in a company this part also needs some training uh usually monks get not used to teaching dhamma to a crowd of people and initially you will have a lot of fear when you face a crowd of people so it takes practice and slowly to get used to teaching to many people it's number 6 then number 7 is discipline that means for the the discipline that is taught to or next sticks those who are monks and nuns and because this vinaya is important for a monk to understand so that he knows how to conduct himself otherwise if a person is not knowledgeable about vidaya he conducts himself like a lay person and people by noticing his conduct of his department people might be sort of put out so it is uh important for a mom or none to know how to conduct himself that is taught in the vinaya and then after that he should uh he's used to be a dweller a lodger in solitude and this is for those who want to attain my liberation to leave samsara and it is good if they could stay alone but then also needs a training if you don't have a certain degree of samadhi of concentration and it's difficult to stay alone alone then after that he's encouraged to attain the 4 janus so if he stays in solitude the chances of obtaining the janas are much higher then after that and that leads to release by mind and released by wisdom and that's attaining good so these are the qualities that amanda is encouraged to develop that's why a lot of the things in the sutras are very practical instructions as we study the sutasa we know how to practice the holy life

complete in 5 factors monks among who follows after and resorts to the lodging place which is complete in 5 factors in no long time by destroying the asavas realizes in the same visible existence the release by mind the release by wisdom thoroughly comprehending it of himself and having attained it abides therein i stopped here so here the buddha is saying that a monk should have 5 factors and then he should stay in a lodging place a dwelling which also has another 5 factors then he can attain the liberation by mind and liberation by wisdom so these are very important um conditions like if monk wants to attain liberation round of beavers these are the factors that he should know just now i didn't mention by destroying the ah sabahs here they translate i think a better translation is uncontrolled mental outflows the mind flows because literally means outflows and mental outflows means even the consciousness flowing it's because the consciousness keeps flowing and flowing that is why the whole world is created in our consciousness so when the asavas are destroyed now the mind doesn't flow anymore then the world doesn't arise in consciousness and that's the end of samasara around rebirth and then the buddha continue how is among complete in 5 factors herein a mom is a believer or a mom has faith he believes he is an exalted 1 [Music] perfect in knowledge and practice a welfare a world known trainer unsurpassed of men to be trained teacher of devas and mankind awaken 1 exalted 1 so this is the first factor the monk has to have faith that the buddha is enlightened then moreover he is little troubled by sickness and disease he is furnished with digestive power not too hot or too cold but even and suitable for striving number 3 he is honest no deceiver he is 1 who shows himself as he really is to the teacher to his discerning companions in the holy life number 4 he dwells resolute in energy ever striving to abandon bad qualities stout and strong to acquire good qualities not shirking the burden in good qualities number 5 he is a man of wisdom possessed of wisdom for seeing the rise and fall of things wisdom that is aryan penetrating going on to the utter destruction of ill in this way amongst among is complete in 5 factors and how is 1's lodging place complete in 5 factors here in mountains a lodging place is not too far and not too near but suitable for coming and going by day not frequented by night quiet and undisturbed by noise not plagued with contact of flies mosquitoes wind rain and creeping things moreover for 1 dwelling in such a lodging place the supply of robes and arms to of bend and lodging comforts in sickness and medicaments comes about with little trouble further in that lodging place 12 elder monks who have heard much who are versed in the sayings who know the teachings thoroughly who know the monastic discipline and summaries by heart consorting with them from time to time he inquires of them and questions them thus how is this your reverence what is the meaning of this those wordies then open up to him what was sealed make clear what was obscure and on diverse doubtful points of doctrine they resolved his doubts that monks is how 1's lodging place is complete in 5 factors indeed monks complete in 5 factors among who follows after and resorts to the lodging place complete also in 5 factors in no long time by destroying the ah sabas realizes in the same visible existence the release by mind the release by wisdom thoroughly comprehending it of himself and having attained it abides therein as the end of the suta
commentary: so the 5 factors a monk should have is that first he must have faith that the buddha is enlightened number 2 he must be healthy and healthy and strong not sickly so that he can strive energetically number 3 he must be honest honest to his teacher and to his companions he must not be the type of person who is always hiding his bad qualities he must show himself as he really is to his teacher to his companion so that they can help him by criticizing him we can only improve if we are willing to change ourselves if we are not willing to change ourselves we can never improve and that is why this is important we must not hide our bad qualities we must lend others know also that they can criticize us then number 4 that 1 must be energetic and striving uh striving to abandon his bad qualities and to acquire good qualities and number 5 we must have wisdom wisdom to be able to see how things arise and pass away arise and pass away how things in this world all arise and pass away that there is nothing stable in this world nothing we can hold on to everything has to arise and pass away so these are the 5 qualities that mom must personally have then he must stay in a place which has another 5 qualities firstly that lodging place must be a suitable place for a monk like a forest monastery is very good if a monk has stayed in the forest by himself alone you'll find that if you stay in the forest by yourself alone sometimes you are disturbed by insects applies a place where you're sleeping you might be sleeping on top of an s in the middle of the night you get bitten by ants all over anybody can't even see properly so sometimes you sleep on the ground in the forest the snake might come and crawl in into where you're sleeping so all these things will disturb you but if you sleep in in uh then you can practice much better that's why the forest monasteries are very suitable for striving because it has these qualities and it's not too far not too near and not too many people come during the day time and night is quiet etc then the second quality you must that monk must be able to get the supply of requisites food robes medicines and a proper dwelling place so these must be suitable number 3 the place where he stays should have elder monks who have heard much elder monster means that refers to a monk who has who has taken the higher ordination at least 10 years 10 years or more and here he says the elder monks must be those who have heard much that means those who are very familiar with the suitables why why doesn't he mention that those elder monks who are very good in meditation and here this is more important if the monk is very familiar with the suit then he has right view and if he has right views he can bring the other younger monks to have right view and right view is the most important factor in the noble hateful path and that's why it is put as the first factor of the noble hateful path only when you attain right view 1 have you entered the noble eightfold path and you have not attained right view you have not entered the noble eightfold path also a person who has attained right view he is already an arya in the personal right view is an arya so once you enter an arya then you are already secure you are safe suttas that's why the monastery has senior monks who are much learned who know the sutas very well and discipline also very well then they can teach the younger monks to have right view so there's the third condition and the fourth 1 that monk must [Music] 5 those monks must be willing to resolve his doubts by answering him guiding him in the dhamma and vinaya so these are the 5 and 5 are important conditions the first 5 important conditions that the monk must have firstly he must have faith faith in the enlightenment of the buddha number 2 he must be strong and healthy so that he can strive for example if you stay alone in the forest for a month to stay alone in the forest he has to stand the cold sometimes you sleep on the back floor uh the floor can be very cold sometimes you sleep in a cave you can have a wind blowing you're not strong enough you cannot stand and sometimes you go and beg for the food sometimes you might get food that does not suit you sometimes you may not get enough food so if your body is not strong but you cannot stand all these things so i know that mom has to be strong then number 3 he has to be honest show himself as he really is to his teacher and to his companions so that he can get guidance and number 4 he must be willing to strive energetic in striving and number 5 he must have a certain amount of wisdom amount of wisdom and to attain insight so these are the 5 personal factors that he should have then the other 5 factors should stay in a lodging place now which is suitable which is quiet and undisturbed not too many people come to disturb not too far not too near etc and number 2 the requisition that are unnecessary food robes etc he can get easily number 3 the dwelling place should have elder monks who number 4 he should be able to ask them about dhamma and vinaya number 5 they must be willing to uh help him overcome all his doubts and all these questions so these are the 5 and the next term is
